sourceMovie.title: Lift
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Lift (2024) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ocean's Eight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Lift clicked for you, Ocean's Eight (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Lift?
faq.answer: Try Ocean's Eight, Red Notice, It's a Mad, Mad, Mad, Mad World—each shares DNA with Lift while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Lift?
faq.answer: Lift (2024) is best described as Action, Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Lift worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
